content: Amines are byproducts of protein degradation and microbial decarboxylation, causing allergic responses.
Histamine is one type of amine, which can cause swelling and itching within the body.
Cooking proteins every two days can reduce amine production and improve nutrient absorption.
Meats, fish, red meats, and cheeses are common sources of amines that can trigger allergic reactions.
Individuals with digestive issues such as leaky gut or candida may be more susceptible to amine-related problems.
Red meat and fish spoil faster due to higher amine content, emphasizing the importance of cooking these foods more frequently.
